qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: CalDAV auth tokens stay usable after the account enters a blocked email-confirmation state
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a backend CalDAV authentication-state bypass in `pkg/routes/caldav/auth.go`; the advisory is a stored XSS in `frontend/src/components/tasks/partials/TaskGlanceTooltip.vue` when task descriptions are inserted into `innerHTML` for the hover preview.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Read-only link-share holders can disclose sibling share hashes and mint stronger share tokens
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a backend link-share authorization flaw in `pkg/models/link_sharing.go` and `pkg/routes/api/v1/link_sharing_auth.go`; the advisory root cause is frontend stored XSS in the task preview renderer, so the vulnerable component, sink, and control flow do not align.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the final qualifying findings identifies the advisory's stored-XSS root cause in the task-preview hover flow. The retained findings are backend authorization flaws on the CalDAV and link-share surfaces, not the frontend `TaskGlanceTooltip.vue` description-rendering path.
evidence: The advisory describes stored XSS in `frontend/src/components/tasks/partials/TaskGlanceTooltip.vue` where task description HTML is written into `innerHTML` for the task preview.
The `caldav_token_state_bypass` finding is anchored in `pkg/routes/caldav/auth.go`, `pkg/user/token.go`, and account-state validation logic for stale CalDAV token authentication.
The `link_share_hash_privilege_escalation` finding is anchored in `pkg/models/link_sharing.go`, `pkg/routes/api/v1/link_sharing_auth.go`, and share JWT minting from disclosed sibling hashes.
The session checklist explicitly characterizes the retained findings as backend-default runtime issues and not frontend findings.
